title: Pentacalia carpishensis
intro: Pentacalia carpishensis, a species of flowering plant in the aster family, is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia carpishensis include pentacyclic triterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pentacalia carpishensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in traditional medicine practices.
taxonomy: Pentacalia carpishensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Asteraceae, and is a part of the genus Pentacalia. This classification places it within the broader group of flowering plants and more specifically within the group of daisy-like plants. Pentacalia carpishensis is further classified within the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. The kingdom Plantae is the highest taxonomic level to which Pentacalia carpishensis belongs. Pentacalia carpishensis's classification is part of the larger effort to organize and understand plant diversity.
uses: Pentacalia carpishensis is a plant species native to Peru, and its medicinal uses have been studied in recent years.
